decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to aggressively control the tempo of the game by efficiently using removal spells and disruptive cards while deploying threats that can quickly close out the game. The deck aims to quickly establish board presence with cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze. The mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, with a mix of fetch lands and dual lands to ensure the deck can operate smoothly and efficiently.

The deck focuses on utilizing Show and Tell to cheat big threats like Emrakul and Archon of Cruelty onto the battlefield quickly. The combo is supported by cards like Sneak Attack to ensure these creatures can have immediate impact. The deck also includes disruption in the form of counterspells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo. The mana base is designed to support the combo with efficient lands like Ancient Tomb and City of Traitors. Synergy is also present with Atraxa, Grand Unifier to help stabilize the board and Stock Up to filter through the deck.

This deck revolves around using the Doomsday combo to manipulate and stack the top 5 cards of your library in a way that allows you to win the game on the spot. It uses card draw and filtering to set up the combo quickly, while also including disruption like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect your game plan. The deck also includes some value cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to help generate mana and speed up your game plan. The goal is to quickly assemble the necessary cards to win the game in one explosive turn.

The main theme of the deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while generating value through controlling the board with cards like Smallpox and Malevolent Rumble. The deck utilizes the synergy between cards like Eumidian Hatchery, Life from the Loam, and Grist, the Hunger Tide to create a resource advantage over time. Additionally, the deck includes disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Inquisition of Kozilek to further disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also utilizes land destruction with cards like Wasteland and Ghost Quarter to further limit the opponent's resources.

The main focus of this deck is to achieve tempo advantage by disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Wasteland, while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent. The deck aims to control the game through disruption and removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out, while also maintaining card advantage and selection with cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Brainstorm. The deck combines disruption, aggression, and control elements to outmaneuver opponents and secure victories.

This deck focuses on utilizing the powerful combo of Doomsday and Thassa's Oracle to win the game quickly. By utilizing cards like Brainstorm, Force of Will, and Daze to control the game and protect the combo pieces, the goal is to set up a winning pile with Doomsday that allows Thassa's Oracle to win the game on the spot. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Murktide Regent to disrupt opponents' game plans while advancing its own win condition.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling a combo to win the game in one explosive turn. It utilizes a combination of aggressive mana acceleration, hand disruption, and graveyard manipulation to quickly deploy a critical mass of spells and creatures. By efficiently using resources and carefully timing card plays, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents and win the game before they have a chance to respond. The key strategy is to assemble the necessary combo pieces as quickly as possible while disrupting the opponent's game plan to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on locking down the opponent by utilizing artifacts and control elements. By combining cards like Urza's Tower, Karn, the Great Creator, and Trinisphere, it aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and stall the game until it can establish a win condition with powerful artifacts like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and The One Ring. The deck also utilizes cards like Urza's Saga and Planar Nexus to generate value and maintain control over the game. Overall, it is a synergistic artifact prison deck designed to control the board and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats.

The deck focuses on a fast-paced aggressive strategy by using disruptive creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Skyclave Apparition to disrupt opponents' strategies while putting pressure on them with efficient creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. The deck also utilizes tools like Aether Vial and Stoneforge Mystic to efficiently deploy threats and disrupt opponents' mana bases with cards like Ghost Quarter and Wasteland. Additionally, the deck includes powerful equipment like Umezawa's Jitte and Meteor Sword to further enhance the aggressive game plan.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan by utilizing a combination of hand disruption, land destruction, and recursion in order to control the board and create a resource advantage. It plays a variety of threats and responses to maintain control of the game while gradually grinding out advantage through attrition. The deck's strategy revolves around generating value from sacrificing and recurring lands, leading to a slow but inevitable victory by outlasting the opponent's resources and preventing them from executing their own game plan effectively.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, then bring them back into play using cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead. This allows you to cheat expensive creatures into play early and overwhelm your opponent with powerful threats. The deck also includes disruption spells like Unmask and Thoughtseize to protect your game plan and remove threats from your opponent's hand. Overall, the deck focuses on combining reanimation spells with powerful creatures to create a strong combo strategy.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan. Utilizing cheap spells like Ponder, Daze, and Lightning Bolt to control the board early on, it aims to pressure the opponent with aggressive creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Wasteland and Force of Will to keep the opponent off balance. By efficiently managing resources and maintaining a fast pace, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their own game plan.

This deck revolves around assembling the combo pieces needed to cast Doomsday and win the game by utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Brainstorm to set up a winning pile. The deck also includes disruption cards like Daze, Thoughtseize, and Force of Will to protect the combo and interact with the opponent. Thassa's Oracle is the primary win condition, with Flow State as an alternate win condition. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure a victory.

This UR Tempo deck focuses on aggressively disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Wasteland and Boomerang Basics while deploying threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Cori-Steel Cutter to pressure the opponent quickly. With cheap interaction in the form of Lightning Bolt and Daze, along with disruption like Force of Will, the deck aims to maintain control of the game while maintaining a fast clock. The deck's key strategy is to efficiently disrupt the opponent while deploying threats to close out the game quickly.

The main strategy of this deck is to quickly generate mana and card advantage using spells like Dark Ritual and Mishra's Bauble to build up a storm count. By utilizing Wishclaw Talisman to search for key combo pieces like Ad Nauseam or Tendrils of Agony, the deck aims to assemble a lethal storm combo to win the game in a single explosive turn. Cards like Urza's Saga and Infernal Tutor provide additional consistency and flexibility in finding the necessary pieces to execute the storm combo efficiently and effectively.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to lock down the opponent's resources and control the game. By utilizing cards like Urza's Saga, Candelabra of Tawnos, and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck aims to establish a powerful board presence while limiting the opponent's ability to effectively play spells. Key pieces like Trinisphere and Planar Nexus further disrupt the opponent's strategy, allowing the player to eventually win the game through overwhelming advantage and resource denial. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, Eye of the Storms to secure victory.
